reason: The score and justifications are well-supported by fan/critic consensus. The Potterwatch omission, the Grimmauld Place confrontation cut (Lupin abandoning Tonks/baby), the reduction of his arc in DH, and his death being glossed over are all widely documented complaints. The Shrieking Shack scene being mostly preserved is also accurate. The main reason it's not a 9-10 is that the motivations score (11/25) and arc score (10/25) feel slightly harsh given that David Thewlis's performance is generally praised as faithful to the character's core personality - the cuts are real but the film Lupin is still recognizable and sympathetic. The personality score (19/25) is the most defensible. Overall the reasoning is factually accurate with no false claims.
